description:
To properly set the owner of /etc/passwd-, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /etc/passwd-
rationale:
The <tt>/etc/passwd-</tt> file is a backup file of <tt>/etc/passwd</tt>, and as such,
it contains information about the users that are configured on the system.
Protection of this file is critical for system security.
ocil_clause: /etc/passwd- does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /etc/passwd-, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/passwd-
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/passwd- to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/passwd-
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/passwd- file must be group-owned by root.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/passwd- file must be owned by root.
stig.vuldiscussion: The "/etc/passwd-" file is a backup file of "/etc/passwd", and as such, contains information about the users that are configured on the system. Protection of this file is critical for system security.
stig.checktext:
Verify the ownership of the "/etc/passwd-" file with the following command:

$ sudo stat -c "%U %n" /etc/passwd-

root /etc/passwd-

If "/etc/passwd-" file does not have an owner of "root", this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Change the owner of the file /etc/passwd- to root by running the following command:

$ sudo chown root /etc/passwd-

title: Verify User Who Owns Backup passwd File
